data.content_ref: inline:The target's per-question strict-beat condition is mathematically incomplete: it requires r_w(q)*n_wrong(q) > n_wrong(q) - n_correct(q) on the SC-wrong question, but this only checks that the harness can flip that question's majority from wrong to right. It omits the necessary condition that the harness must NOT simultaneously break any SC-correct question — yet the 'for all other q' clause only constrains r_c(q')*n_correct(q') < n_correct(q') - n_wrong(q'), which is the condition for NOT breaking each individual question, not the condition for net strict improvement. If the harness fixes the one SC-wrong question but breaks even one SC-correct question, net fixed-minus-broke is zero, not strictly positive. The target conflates per-question non-breakage with net strict advantage, so the stated iff is false.
